children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a State may use up to 100 percent of any covered funds of the State to repair or replace a transportation facility that has suffered serious damage as a result of a natural disaster or catastrophic failure from an external cause.

children.heading: .—Declaration of Emergency
children.label: b .—Declaration of Emergency
children.text: Funds may be used under this section only for a disaster or emergency declared by the President pursuant to the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 5121

children.heading: .—Repayment
children.label: c .—Repayment
children.text: Funds used under subsection (a) shall be repaid to the program from which the funds were taken in the event that such repairs or replacement are subsequently covered by a supplemental appropriation of funds.

children.heading: .—Covered funds
children.label: 1 .—Covered funds
children.text: The term “covered funds” means any amounts apportioned to a State under section 104(b), other than amounts suballocated to metropolitan areas and other areas of the State under section 133(d), but including any such amounts required to be set aside for a purpose other than the repair or replacement of a transportation facility under this section.

children.heading: .—Transportation facility
children.label: 2 .—Transportation facility
children.text: The term “transportation facility” means any facility eligible for assistance under section 125.
